title: salix × ungavensis
intro: Salix × ungavensis, also known as Ungava Willow, is a hybrid willow tree that is native to the boreal regions of Canada, specifically in the Ungava Peninsula. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain due to its salicylic acid content, which is similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × ungavensis include its potential to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and ease menstrual cramps. Salix × ungavensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
